concept: Climb / Ascend by Grip and Effort
description: To climb: the tree for fruit, the hill for the view, the ladder to the roof. Upward motion earned hold by hold. With 'rihe' (rise) as its effortless cousin: smoke rises, people climb.
sound_symbolism: 'ka' is the grip, 'le' the pull upward, and the final thin 'i' is the high place reached — the word ascends through its own vowels.
grammatical_notes: With object or path: 'lopia shiro kalei.' (The child climbs the tree.) 'lo mia molira kalei.' (We climb the hill.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The climb is progress that cannot be rushed — each hold must be actual before the next is reached for. Ambition, disciplined by physics.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Orchard, roof-thatch, lookout: climbing was daily competence, learned young and kept until old.
